header.occasion: Phase B investigation findings (BUG 2/5 root cause confirmed) + Phase C remediation options

phase_b_findings.bug_5_full_scope: The F-1 fix interaction breaks ALL 5 PowerShell scripts that read `.mcp.json` directly. Python is unaffected because it reads env vars directly, NOT `.mcp.json` templates.
phase_b_findings.affected_powershell_scripts:
All 5 use the IDENTICAL broken pattern: `Get-Content .mcp.json | ConvertFrom-Json` then `$vikPass = $mcp.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS`. NONE expand the `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` template.
Lines 380-383. **Fail-open path → invokes claude on every wake-* firing if Vikunja login fails.** This is BUG 5's primary cost-bleed risk.
Lines 41-52. **Fail-closed path** — exits cleanly on login failure (per cadence_monitor_*.log evidence). No claude invocation. This is BUG 2's diagnosed bug — same root cause as BUG 5 but different failure mode.
Lines 44-56. **Fail-closed** — `Write-Output "ESCALATION-WATCHDOG: failed to parse..."; exit 0`. No claude invocation. But escalation polling is broken — LA won't get escalation toasts (separate from disabled Toast Watchdog issue).
LA-invoked manual script (you trigger from Vikunja escalation paste-to-PowerShell). Same broken pattern. Currently moot because (a) escalation watchdog is broken so no escalations reach you, (b) you'd notice login failure when running manually.
Test/diagnostic script — not triggered by scheduled tasks. Manual invocation only.
phase_b_findings.python_unaffected:
`vikunja_mcp/cli.py` lines 57-59: ```python VIKUNJA_URL = os.environ.get("VIKUNJA_URL", "http://localhost:3456") VIKUNJA_USER = os.environ.get("VIKUNJA_USER", "blarai") VIKUNJA_PASS = os.environ.get("VIKUNJA_PASS", "") ``` Reads env vars directly. F-1 fix doesn't affect Python. **This is why Item 4.11a CLI smoke test passed** — cli.py never touches .mcp.json template.
Other Python files using same pattern (presumed): vikunja_mcp/server.py, vikunja_mcp/bridge/daemon.py, fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py.
phase_b_findings.root_cause_summary: The F-1 fix (n1(g)) replaced `.mcp.json` literal `VIKUNJA_PASS` with `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` template so VS Code's MCP loader could expand at server-launch time. **VS Code's MCP loader handles template expansion. PowerShell's ConvertFrom-Json does NOT.** Direct PowerShell readers see literal string `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` and use it as the password. Vikunja login fails with "Wrong username or password" code 1011.

option.name: REVERT — restore .mcp.json literal password
option.action:
Edit BlarAI/.mcp.json to replace `"VIKUNJA_PASS": "${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}"` with the actual password literal: `"VIKUNJA_PASS": "<actual_password_value>"`.
Source the literal from `[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS', 'User')` (env var IS set per Phase A check; 23 chars).
option.pros: - Single config change. NO PowerShell script modifications needed. - All 5 affected PS1 scripts work without code changes. - VS Code MCP loader still works (reads literal as-is — templates aren't required, just supported). - Python unaffected (still uses env var directly).
option.cons: - Reverts F-1 fix's template format. F-1's stated motivation was "VS Code MCP loader expands at server-launch time" — but expansion is OPTIONAL; literal also works. - Plaintext password in `.mcp.json` (gitignored per Ticket I, so NOT exposed via git). Still on disk. - Future password rotations: must update `.mcp.json` AND env var (sync burden).
option.effort: ~2 min (single Edit operation on one file).

option.name: TEMPLATE EXPANSION — add expansion logic to all 5 PS1 scripts
option.action: Add ONE LINE to each of 5 PS1 scripts after `$vikPass = ...`: ```powershell if ($vikPass -match '^\$\{env:(\w+)\}$') { $vikPass = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable($matches[1]) } ``` Same for $vikUser + $vikUrl if they're also templated (currently only VIKUNJA_PASS is).
option.pros: - Preserves F-1 template format. - Defensive — works whether template is literal or `${env:X}`. - Distributed fix; each script self-contained.
option.cons: - Modifies 5 scripts. - Repetitive (same logic in 5 places). - More effort + more files in commit.

option.name: DIRECT ENV VAR — refactor 5 PS1s to read env vars (Python pattern)
option.pros: - Cleanest design. Matches Python pattern (consistency). - Single source of truth (env var). - No .mcp.json dependency for credentials. - Future password rotations: update env var only.
option.cons: - Larger refactor (5 scripts × multiple-line changes). - Requires VIKUNJA_USER + VIKUNJA_URL env vars to be set as well (currently only VIKUNJA_PASS is set). - More risk of regression than Options A or B.
option.effort: ~30-45 min + Stage 6.7.5 ticket for proper testing.

phase_c_options.recommended_path:
**Phase C immediate (tonight or tomorrow morning)**: Option A — revert .mcp.json to literal. ~2 min. Fixes BUGs 2 + 5 in one change.
**Stage 6.7.5 hardening ticket** (NEW Ticket II): refactor PS1 scripts to read env vars directly per Python pattern. Architectural cleanup; not blocking.
Plus Phase C still needs: - **C2 (BUG 3)**: revert Item 4.7.5.C daily_digest.py:95 (1 file, ~5 min) - **C3 (BUG 4)**: relocate wake_launcher logs to devplatform OR exclude from Stage 5 deletion (LA decision needed) - **C4 (BUG 1 long-term)**: state.json location post-Stage-5 (LA decision needed)

phase_c_execution_recommendation.option_a_execution_block:
```powershell # === Phase C1: Option A — revert .mcp.json VIKUNJA_PASS to literal ===
# Step 1: read current .mcp.json + capture env var value $mcp_path = "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json" $vikunja_pass_value = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS', 'User') if (-not $vikunja_pass_value) { throw "VIKUNJA_PASS env var not set; cannot proceed" } Write-Host "Captured VIKUNJA_PASS env var ($($vikunja_pass_value.Length) chars)"
# Step 2: read .mcp.json, replace template with literal $mcp_content = Get-Content -Raw $mcp_path $current = ($mcp_content | ConvertFrom-Json).mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS Write-Host "Current VIKUNJA_PASS in .mcp.json: $current"
if ($current -ne '${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}') { Write-Host "Already not template — skipping" } else { # Substitute template with literal # Note: JSON requires escaping quotes/backslashes in literal value if any $literal_escaped = $vikunja_pass_value -replace '\\','\\\\' -replace '"','\"' $new_content = $mcp_content -replace '"\$\{env:VIKUNJA_PASS\}"', '"' + $literal_escaped + '"'
# Write back (preserve original encoding) [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($mcp_path, $new_content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
# Verify $new = (Get-Content -Raw $mcp_path | ConvertFrom-Json).mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS if ($new -eq $vikunja_pass_value) { Write-Host "Item C1: .mcp.json VIKUNJA_PASS reverted to literal ✓" -ForegroundColor Green } else { throw "Verify FAIL: expected literal match, got '$new'" } }
# NO STAGING — .mcp.json is gitignored per Ticket I # NO COMMIT ```
After Option A executes: - All 5 PowerShell scripts will work on next firing (no script changes needed) - Vikunja login probe in WORK-GATE will succeed → wake-* tasks correctly skip claude when no work pending - Cadence monitor will start logging successfully - Escalation watchdog will start polling Vikunja correctly

next_steps.option_immediate: Execute Phase C1 (Option A) now — ~2 min — fixes BUGs 2 + 5. Then re-test wake_launcher login (read-only check).
next_steps.option_continue: Phase C2 (BUG 3 daily_digest.py:95 revert), C3 (BUG 4 logs location), C4 (BUG 1 long-term).
next_steps.option_unpause: After Phase C complete + verified: unpause fleet again (Phase D).
next_steps.option_pause_here: If LA wants to wind down for the night, Phase C tomorrow morning. Fleet stays paused; no claude invocations; $0 overnight cost. This is your previously-stated overnight plan.
